6_phase_9_amadeus_mcp.notes: MCP tools/list and get_token succeeded; get_token reported token_bound=true without exposing token material in this result. Actual search_locations/search_flights calls failed with Tool returned error and shaped zero result counts.

failure_modes.notes: OpenAI classified flights correctly, then Amadeus branch failed with empty Bearer token.
classify_intent failed with Illegal header value b'Bearer '; parser fell through to help render but execution remained FAILED.
classify_intent failed with Request error after roughly 60s; parser fell through to help render but execution remained FAILED.
classify_intent returned Tool returned error; parser fell through to help render but execution remained FAILED.

regressions_found.name: travel runtime Amadeus branch has empty keychain.amadeus_token.access_token
regressions_found.details: The SQL quoting regression is fixed, but flights and locations still fail because the travel runtime's Amadeus HTTP headers render to Bearer with an empty token. Evidence: executions 622746596920262831 and 622746596869931182 both reached log_classification and then failed in amadeus_search_flights/amadeus_search_locations with Illegal header value b'Bearer '. In contrast, the standalone automation/agents/mcp/amadeus get_token execution 622747225470271832 completed and reported token_bound=true, so the credentials exist and the issue is specific to the travel runtime/keychain path.

regressions_found.name: provider-specific keychain predicates do not validate cleanly for non-openai travel providers
regressions_found.details: OpenAI help completed, but vertex-ai and anthropic provider switches failed during classify_intent, and ollama timed out/fell through. This likely needs a focused follow-up on keychain `when:` handling and provider URL/header rendering in the travel runtime.

regressions_found.name: Amadeus MCP search calls fail despite token_bound=true
regressions_found.details: MCP tools/list returned tool_count=5 and get_token returned token_bound=true, but search_locations and search_flights executions failed with Tool returned error and shaped zero results. This may be an Amadeus API request-shape issue or response/error propagation issue in the new MCP playbook.

notes: The one allowed ops PR was merged and the local catalog now uses travel runtime version 2.
The original AMBER blocker is fixed: log_classification no longer fails on the help path.
This round cannot be GREEN because the required flights/locations, canvas, MCP search, and provider-switch smokes are not all passing.
No validation log entry was appended because verdict is AMBER.
deferred_followups: Focused ops/noetl investigation: why travel runtime keychain does not provide amadeus_token for Amadeus branches while automation/agents/mcp/amadeus get_token succeeds.
Focused ops investigation: Amadeus MCP search_locations/search_flights return Tool returned error despite token_bound=true.
Provider-switch parity: validate keychain `when:` predicates for openai, anthropic, vertex-ai, and ollama once the runtime keychain issue is understood.
Phase 2: travel agent uses the Amadeus MCP server internally.
